channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Company
item.description: A company has updated its Responsible Scaling Policy to enhance risk management for frontier AI systems, introducing flexible thresholds for safeguards. This change aims to better mitigate potential catastrophic risks while ensuring responsible AI deployment.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion aims to enhance the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring sessions on agentic AI and Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering improved GPU and NPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance the efficiency of deploying advanced AI models.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud has introduced Wednesday Build Hour, a weekly interactive session for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. This initiative aims to provide practical skills and immediate application in workflows.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and custom commands. These improvements aim to enhance collaboration between developers and AI tools.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has introduced new features in Gemini Code Assist for IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce developer friction by automating code completion and providing interactive summaries. These tools aim to enhance coding efficiency.

item.title: Chatbots are great at manipulating people to buy stuff, Princeton boffins find
item.description: Research from Princeton indicates that chatbots can effectively manipulate consumer behavior, raising concerns about ethical advertising practices. The study calls for restraint to ensure transparency in AI-driven marketing.

item.title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100/month Pro plan for ChatGPT, addressing user demand for a mid-tier subscription option. This plan aims to provide more flexibility for power users.

item.title: I tried CuerdOS and this niche Debian distro is dramatically fast
item.description: CuerdOS is a new Linux distribution that stands out for its speed and unique approach to preinstalled software, making it a compelling option for users seeking performance.

item.title: Florida AG announces investigation into OpenAI over shooting that allegedly involved ChatGPT
item.description: Florida's Attorney General is investigating OpenAI after ChatGPT was allegedly used to plan a shooting, leading to potential lawsuits from victims' families.

item.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
item.description: A Western Union executive claims that negative perceptions of Broadcom are driving many VMware migrations, indicating challenges in their partnership.

item.title: After data breach, $10B-valued startup Mercor is having a month
item.description: Mercor, a startup valued at $10 billion, is facing lawsuits and losing clients after a significant data breach, impacting its reputation and operations.

item.title: Ask Copilot in security assessments now available
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature allowing organization admins to use Copilot directly in security assessments, enhancing contextual understanding and guidance.

item.title: Anthropic will let your agents sleep on its couch
item.description: Anthropic has launched Managed Agents, a service designed to automate business tasks using AI agents, potentially transforming operational workflows.

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: Meta's AI app has surged to No. 5 on the App Store after the launch of its Muse Spark model, indicating strong user interest and engagement.

item.title: Google wants more Intel inside ... its datacenters, taps Chipzilla for more SmartNICs
item.description: Google has decided to continue its partnership with Intel, purchasing SmartNICs for its data centers, contrasting with AWS's independent approach.

item.title: ChatGPT has a new $100 per month Pro subscription
item.description: OpenAI has launched a new $100 per month Pro subscription for ChatGPT, offering significantly more usage of its Codex tool compared to the previous Plus tier.

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Anthropic has limited the release of its new model, Mythos, citing concerns over its ability to exploit software vulnerabilities, raising questions about its motivations.

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: Google and Intel are collaborating to develop custom chips amid a global CPU shortage, aiming to enhance AI infrastructure capabilities. This partnership highlights the increasing demand for advanced computing solutions.

item.title: Home Depot will give you a free power tool with this DeWalt deal - what to know
item.description: Home Depot is offering a promotion where customers can receive a free power tool with the purchase of a DeWalt bundle, encouraging early engagement in lawn care projects. This deal aims to attract DIY enthusiasts.

item.title: Crypto? Huh. Good gawd y'all, what is it good for? $45M in this case
item.description: Law enforcement in the US, UK, and Canada disrupted a $45 million global cryptocurrency scam, recovering $12 million for victims and identifying over 20,000 fraudulent wallet addresses. This operation underscores the ongoing issues with crypto-related fraud.

item.title: Florida launches investigation into OpenAI
item.description: Florida's Attorney General has initiated an investigation into OpenAI, citing concerns over public safety and national security risks related to its technology potentially falling into the hands of adversaries. This raises significant questions about AI governance.

item.title: Samsung S95H vs. Samsung S95F: I compared both OLED TVs, and there's a surprise winner
item.description: A comparison of Samsung's S95H and S95F OLED TVs reveals surprising performance differences, prompting consumers to reconsider their upgrade decisions. This analysis helps buyers make informed choices in a competitive market.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs, a small AI image startup, is making strides in the competitive AI image generation field and plans to expand into physical AI applications. Their growth reflects the potential of smaller firms in the tech landscape.

item.title: World's smallest violin spotted at Amazon HQ as exec pay packets deflate
item.description: Amazon executives are facing reduced pay compared to previous years, leading to humorous commentary about their new frugal lifestyle. This reflects broader trends in corporate compensation amid economic pressures.

item.title: New Low Quality option in the Hide comment menu
item.description: GitHub has introduced a 'Low Quality' classification for hidden comments, enhancing moderation tools for users managing discussions and contributions. This feature aims to improve the quality of interactions on the platform.

item.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
item.description: Amazon Bedrock's new post outlines how to manage the lifecycle of AI models, ensuring applications remain functional as models evolve. This guidance is crucial for developers working with AI technologies.

item.title: AWS: Agents shouldn't be secret, so we built a registry for them
item.description: AWS has launched a registry for AI agents to enhance visibility and management of software automations in corporate settings. This initiative aims to improve transparency in automated processes.

item.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
item.description: AWS has launched a preview of the AWS Agent Registry, allowing enterprises to discover, share, and reuse AI agents and tools efficiently. This development signifies a step towards more streamlined management of AI resources at scale.

item.title: New Sort by control added to Notifications
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new 'Sort by' control on the Notifications page, allowing users to organize notifications from newest to oldest or vice versa. This update improves user experience by providing more control over notification viewing.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Sierra's Ghostwriter tool aims to revolutionize app development by allowing users to create agents through natural language commands instead of traditional clicking. This shift could significantly change how applications are built and interacted with.

item.title: How I turned my Android tablet into the ultimate Kindle alternative - for free
item.description: A guide explains how to turn an Android tablet into a Kindle alternative, allowing users to access their entire reading library on one device. This offers a cost-effective solution for avid readers.

item.title: Repository member role labels now in pull request list view
item.description: GitHub has added repository member role labels to the pull request list view, making it easier to identify contributors' roles directly. This update enhances clarity and collaboration within public repositories.

item.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
item.description: This article provides a tutorial on embedding a live AI browser agent in a React app using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. It outlines the steps to create an interactive experience for users.

item.title: Apple's iOS 26.4.1 update enables Stolen Device Protection by default now - grab it today
item.description: Apple's latest iOS update (26.4.1) introduces Stolen Device Protection by default and fixes a glitch with iCloud syncing. This update enhances device security and user experience.

item.title: I use ChatGPT's new Tubi app to find free movies and TV shows to watch - here's how
item.description: The Tubi TV streaming service now integrates with ChatGPT, allowing users to search for free movies and TV shows using AI. This feature enhances the user experience by simplifying content discovery.

item.title: Why Ubuntu 26.04 will thrill gamers - and it's not just the performance bump
item.description: Ubuntu 26.04 is set to excite gamers with performance improvements and new applications, making it a compelling choice for gaming enthusiasts. The update also addresses some missing features that enhance overall user experience.

item.title: Release information in issue sidebar and default values for project fields
item.description: GitHub's latest update includes release tracking in the issue sidebar and default values for project fields, streamlining navigation and improving project management. This enhancement aims to facilitate better tracking of issues and releases.

item.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
item.description: Meta has modernized WebRTC to avoid the pitfalls of forking, ensuring it remains aligned with community upgrades while supporting over 50 use cases. This modernization enhances real-time audio and video capabilities across their platforms.

item.title: The best Android tablets of 2026: Lab tested, expert recommended
item.description: ZDNET has tested and recommended the best Android tablets for 2026, showcasing options from brands like Samsung and TCL. This guide helps consumers navigate the tablet market beyond just iPads.

item.title: OpenAI puts Stargate UK on ice, blames energy costs and red tape
item.description: OpenAI has paused its Stargate datacenter project in the UK due to high energy costs and regulatory challenges. This decision reflects the difficulties tech companies face in expanding infrastructure in certain regions.

item.title: Introducing stateful MCP client capabilities on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime
item.description: Amazon Bedrock has introduced stateful MCP client capabilities, allowing for dynamic content generation and user interaction during execution. This advancement enhances the functionality of AI applications on their platform.

item.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
item.description: The game 'Samson: A Tyndalston Story' has been released on GeForce NOW, allowing users to stream it on various devices. This release showcases the potential of cloud gaming for delivering cinematic experiences.

item.title: Security researchers tricked Apple Intelligence into cursing at users. It could have been a lot worse
item.description: Security researchers have demonstrated that Apple Intelligence can be manipulated through prompt injection, potentially exposing millions of users to risks. This vulnerability raises concerns about AI security in consumer devices.

item.title: The AI industry’s race for profits is now existential
item.description: The AI industry faces a monetization cliff, as companies struggle to turn profits before potential collapse. This discussion highlights the urgent need for sustainable business models in AI.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is showcasing advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their transformative impact across various industries. This highlights the rapid evolution of robot capabilities.

item.title: GitHub availability report: March 2026
item.description: GitHub's availability report for March 2026 details four incidents that led to degraded service performance. This transparency is important for users relying on GitHub for their projects.

item.title: CyberAgent moves faster with ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex
item.description: CyberAgent is leveraging ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance AI adoption and decision-making in advertising, media, and gaming sectors. This integration aims to improve operational efficiency.
